Function: Central access point for High Plains climate tools, data, and reports.
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, ag extension agents, planners, emergency managers
Impact Evidence: Central access point for High Plains climate data; referenced in NE, KS, CO, ND, SD, WY state climatologist workflows
Notes: Primary HPRCC portal; ACIS-backed; loss would sever regional access to the full tool suite

Function: Generates temperature, precipitation, and anomaly maps over custom timeframes.
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, planners, ag extension agents
Impact Evidence: Referenced in High Plains drought monitoring and agricultural situation reports
Notes: Custom timeframe ACIS map generator; core monitoring product; regional framing not replicated nationally

Function: Provides station-level climate data queries and export options.
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, data analysts, developers
Notes: Station-level data query and export; ACIS-backed; CLIMOD2 and cli-MATE are partial substitutes at other centers

Function: Displays summary statistics for temperature, precipitation, and degree days.
User Segment: Researchers, planners, ag extension agents, media
Notes: Climate summary statistics by station; ACIS-backed; moderate standalone restoration priority

Function: Weekly summary of temperature and precipitation anomalies across the region.
User Segment: Farmers, ag extension agents, state climatologists, emergency managers, media
Impact Evidence: Weekly summaries distributed to USDA FSA High Plains offices and state ag departments
Notes: Weekly anomaly monitoring product; no direct regional substitute; operationally embedded in Plains ag workflows

Function: Central hub for agriculture-related maps and products.
User Segment: Farmers, ranchers, ag extension agents, crop consultants, livestock producers
Impact Evidence: Referenced in UNL extension ag decision support materials
Notes: Hub for all HPRCC ag tools; AWDN integration makes this distinct from other RCC ag portals

Function: Calculates livestock heat stress based on temperature, humidity, and wind.
User Segment: Cattle producers, ranchers, livestock managers, feedlot operators
Impact Evidence: Embedded in Nebraska and Kansas cattle producer extension guides; used by feedlot operators for heat stress management
Notes: Unique livestock heat stress tool; no national equivalent at AWDN station resolution; High Plains cattle industry dependency

Function: Maps of cumulative growing degree days for user-defined time windows.
User Segment: Farmers, crop consultants, ag extension agents, agronomists
Impact Evidence: Standard reference in High Plains corn and sorghum production extension guides
Notes: GDD maps with custom time windows; ACIS-backed; Corn Belt RCCs have similar but Plains-specific crop calendar is distinct

Function: Daily and cumulative freeze–thaw cycles to assess soil and crop impacts.
User Segment: Farmers, ag extension agents, crop consultants, infrastructure managers
Notes: Daily and cumulative freeze-thaw cycle tracker; unique at High Plains station resolution; no national equivalent

Function: Near-surface soil temperature and moisture conditions from AWDN stations.
User Segment: Farmers, irrigation managers, ag extension agents, NRCS staff, conservation districts
Impact Evidence: Referenced in NRCS irrigation scheduling guidance for High Plains; embedded in Nebraska irrigation management workflows
Notes: AWDN-sourced soil data; entirely distinct from ACIS; loss of AWDN = loss of this tool entirely; highest unique infrastructure risk at HPRCC

Function: Maps of cumulative precipitation deficits used for drought tracking.
User Segment: Farmers, drought monitors, ag extension agents, water managers
Impact Evidence: Used in High Plains drought early warning workflows by USDA FSA and NDMC
Notes: Precipitation deficit mapping; ACIS-backed; NDMC is partial substitute but HPRCC version optimized for Plains ag

Function: Computes soil water availability using rainfall and evapotranspiration data.
User Segment: Farmers, irrigation managers, conservation districts, water resource planners
Impact Evidence: Referenced in UNL extension irrigation scheduling publications for the Ogallala Aquifer region
Notes: Water balance tool with AWDN ET integration; unique for Plains irrigation context; secondary data pipeline adds restoration complexity

Function: Displays SPI, precipitation percent-of-normal, and drought severity categories.
User Segment: Emergency managers, water resource managers, farmers, state agencies, NDMC
Impact Evidence: Feeds into NDMC drought monitor products; referenced in High Plains state drought contingency plans
Notes: SPI and drought severity dashboard; NDMC is partial substitute nationally; HPRCC version provides Plains-specific resolution and ACIS integration

Function: Provides ENSO phase summaries and regional impacts for the High Plains.
User Segment: Farmers, seasonal forecasters, ag extension agents, water managers
Notes: ENSO phase and regional impact summaries; NOAA CPC provides national context; HPRCC version Plains-framed for ag applications

Function: Shows long-term station trends for temperature and precipitation.
User Segment: Researchers, planners, policymakers, educators
Notes: Long-term trend visualization; NOAA Climate Explorer partial substitute; HPRCC version Plains-specific

Function: Maps of current snow depth and snow water equivalent.
User Segment: Water resource managers, hydrologists, emergency managers, NWS forecasters
Notes: Snow depth and SWE maps; NRCS SNOTEL provides national data; HPRCC interface provides Plains-specific framing and ACIS integration

Function: Written summaries of weekly climate highlights across the region.
User Segment: Planners, state agencies, media, researchers, extension agents
Notes: Weekly narrative reports; staff-authored component limits automated restoration; maps are ACIS-recoverable

Function: Historical precipitation normals and anomalies for the High Plains.
User Segment: Hydrologists, water resource planners, researchers, ag extension agents
Notes: Historical precip normals and anomalies; overlaps with ACIS map products (same URL); verify as distinct page

Function: Regional streamflow summaries and hydrologic context.
User Segment: Water resource managers, hydrologists, emergency managers, municipal water utilities
Notes: Streamflow summaries with hydrologic context; USGS provides national streamflow data; HPRCC regional synthesis is the value-add

Function: Map viewer for monthly, seasonal, and annual precipitation totals.
User Segment: Hydrologists, planners, farmers, water resource managers
Notes: Monthly and seasonal precipitation map viewer; shares URL with ACIS Climate Maps — verify as distinct page

Function: Primary data access point for AWDN station network observations.
User Segment: Farmers, irrigation managers, ag extension agents, researchers, NRCS staff
Impact Evidence: AWDN cited in USDA NRCS irrigation management guidance for the Central Plains; embedded in Nebraska extension workflows
Notes: Primary AWDN data access portal; entirely independent of ACIS; unique High Plains infrastructure; loss would be irreplaceable at regional scale

Function: Visual interface for viewing AWDN station data across the High Plains.
User Segment: Farmers, ag extension agents, researchers, irrigation managers
Notes: Visual AWDN station map interface; unique; no national equivalent for High Plains station network at this resolution

Function: Displays site metadata, history, and sensor configuration.
User Segment: Researchers, network administrators, developers
Notes: AWDN sensor metadata and site history; critical for data provenance and QC; no substitute

Function: Shows AWDN-derived near-surface soil moisture and temperature data.
User Segment: Farmers, irrigation managers, ag extension agents, NRCS staff
Impact Evidence: Embedded in NRCS irrigation scheduling tools for High Plains; referenced in UNL extension soil health publications
Notes: Duplicate entry with row 11 — same URL; verify whether this is a distinct AWDN soil page or same tool listed twice

Function: Climate literacy materials and lesson plans for educators.
User Segment: K-12 educators, students, general public
Notes: Climate literacy materials; many national substitutes exist; low unique restoration value

Function: Case studies and sectoral impact assessments for the High Plains.
User Segment: Planners, policymakers, researchers, educators
Notes: Sectoral impact case studies; archivable; moderate value for evidence base and subdomain content

Function: Short research summaries from applied climate studies at HPRCC.
User Segment: Researchers, educators
Notes: Research summaries; static content; archivable as-is

Function: Lists partner agencies and ongoing cooperative programs.
User Segment: Partner agencies, researchers
Notes: Partner list; static content; low restoration value

Function: Archived site interface containing discontinued tools.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Pre-2020 site archive; mine for discontinued tool inventory; Wayback captures exist

Function: Deprecated map system prior to modernization.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Deprecated map system; superseded by current ACIS maps; archival interest only

Function: Older AWDN pages with static data tables.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Legacy AWDN viewer with static tables; superseded by current AWDN portal; archival interest only

Function: Early anomaly and percent-of-average map tools.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
